advantages: Finds edge cases you didn't think of — the entire point.
Automatic shrinking gives you the minimum reproducer, not the random one.
One property test replaces dozens of example tests for the same surface.
Excellent at stress-testing pure logic (parsers, normalisers, ranking).
Stateful mode finds bug-triggering operation sequences impossible to hand-design.
disadvantages: Requires identifying *properties* — harder than just listing examples.
Slower than example tests (100+ iterations per property).
Can produce noisy failures (e.g. floating-point near-equality on unstable algorithms).
Awkward fit for tests where output is hard to predict (LLM responses, generative models).
Failures often surface real bugs *and* surfaces specification ambiguity — that's good but can derail PRs.
